I couldn't find information about the plant Jacobaeastrum pectinatum. However, based on similar plant names, I can provide information about the plant Jacobaea or Jacobaea pectinata.

Jacobaea pectinata, also known as Jacobaea, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) with a history of traditional use. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Jacobaea pectinata include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Jacobaea pectinata is traditionally used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, often in combination with other plants to enhance its medicinal effects.
